title: AI Landing Page Critique Tool For Indie Founders

input.idea: AI landing page critique tool for indie founders.
input.target_user: Solo founders and indie builders preparing a launch page.
input.use_case: They would use it before Product Hunt, X, Hacker News, or ad traffic to find why visitors will not convert.
input.user_context: User requested a B2B Persona run.

evidence_packet.claim: There is existing interest in expert explanations of SaaS and indie-maker landing pages.
evidence_packet.relevance: Demand is adjacent, but expert-led critique already competes with generic AI feedback.

evidence_packet.claim: Product Hunt asks makers to prepare launch assets and seek comments rather than direct upvotes.
evidence_packet.relevance: Launch prep is a real moment, but the page critique must improve conversation, not just aesthetics.

evidence_packet.claim: Content optimization tools already sell grading, recommendations, and discoverability workflows.
evidence_packet.relevance: A paid critique tool needs a sharper wedge than another scorecard.

evidence_packet.claim: Product Hunt surfaces community threads like landing-page critiques and tagline fixes.
evidence_packet.relevance: Founders seek critique, but often through community feedback that feels human and contextual.

verdict_metrics.name: likely pull
verdict_metrics.reason: Launch pressure is real, but founders already ask peers or ChatGPT

verdict_metrics.name: urgency
verdict_metrics.reason: Highest in the week before a public launch

verdict_metrics.name: willingness to pay
verdict_metrics.reason: Indie founders are price-sensitive unless critique changes conversion

verdict_metrics.name: trust barrier
verdict_metrics.reason: AI critique must prove it understands buyers, not just copy patterns

verdict_metrics.name: differentiation
verdict_metrics.reason: Generic page graders and manual critiques are close substitutes

hard_nos: If it only returns copy tips, founders will use ChatGPT or a free community critique.
If it cannot show buyer-specific objections, the critique will feel like generic CRO advice.
If no before/after examples show better signups, payment will be hard to justify.
If it targets all founders, it will miss the urgent launch-week buyer.
objections_to_beat: Show before/after rewrites for 5 indie launch pages and track whether founders ship the changes.
Prove one narrow promise: finding unclear positioning above the fold, not auditing the whole site.
Compare outputs against 3 human launch reviewers and show where AI catches missed objections.
Test a paid rush critique with founders launching within 14 days, not casual builders.

next_experiment.action: Recruit 10 indie founders with launch pages going live within 30 days; give each a one-page critique plus rewritten hero and ask for $25 after delivery.
next_experiment.why: This tests urgency, trust, and willingness to pay without building a product.
next_experiment.success_signal: At least 3 pay, 5 implement one recommended change, and 2 ask for a second pass.

personas.label: Launch-week solo founder
personas.objection: They need fast outside eyes before traffic arrives.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A concrete rewrite of the hero with predicted objections and launch comments to use.

personas.label: Weekend indie builder
personas.objection: They want feedback but default to free peer channels.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A cheap single-page sample that feels more specific than Discord feedback.

personas.label: Early startup marketer
personas.objection: They already have analytics and messaging docs.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Evidence that critique maps to signup or demo conversion, not taste.

personas.label: Micro-agency owner
personas.objection: They cannot hand clients an untrusted AI critique.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: White-label examples with clear rationale and low hallucination risk.

personas.label: Product lead
personas.objection: Landing copy is not their current bottleneck.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A workflow tied to experiment backlog and observed user objections.

personas.label: Growth lead before campaign
personas.objection: They need measurable lift, not another recommendation list.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A fast test that proposes variants ready for paid traffic.

personas.label: Sales-led founder
personas.objection: They know prospects object, but the page hides those answers.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Critique based on sales-call objections translated into page sections.

personas.label: Skeptical developer-founder
personas.objection: They distrust marketing advice and can prompt an LLM themselves.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A public teardown where the advice is obviously non-obvious.

personas.label: Operations lead
personas.objection: Landing-page critique is outside their job.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A use case tied to internal rollout or vendor evaluation pages.

personas.label: Finance-aware buyer
personas.objection: They will not fund soft conversion advice without a revenue path.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A clear link to campaign spend recovery or demo rate improvement.

personas.label: Indie consultant
personas.objection: They can resell sharper critiques to clients.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: Reusable report format with client-ready language and examples.

personas.label: Enterprise evaluator
personas.objection: Procurement and brand review make a lightweight AI tool irrelevant.
personas.what_would_change_their_mind: A compliance-safe enterprise content workflow, which is a different product.
